rationale.P01: edge-router 5xx tagged SEV1 by the LB rule but it is a health-check flap: 18 fires/hr, self-clears in 34s, metric snaps from 0.21 back to 0.004 the next minute. pat-lb-healthcheck-flap is neutral with ~0% delta. Loud but benign.
rationale.P02: Same edge-router health-check flapper, another oscillation (18 fires/hr, cleared in 29s, metric back to 0.005).
rationale.P03: payments-ledger memory_pct rising monotonically 62->69->77->84->91->96 over ~90m, single fire, never auto-resolves, no deploy. pat-ledger-heap-growth is negative and growing. A leak on a critical stateful service minutes from OOM. SEV2 looks calm but this is must-page.
rationale.P04: recommendation cpu tagged SEV1 but it is a GC-pause spike: 11 fires/hr, self-clears in 26s, metric snaps from 97 back to 46. pat-gc-pause neutral, ~4% delta. Flapper.
rationale.P05: frontend 5xx during fe-2026.03.12-a rollout, auto-resolved in 51s, metric back to 0.003. Deploy transient.
rationale.P06: checkout cpu during the same fe-2026.03.12-a rollout (deployed to checkout too), auto-resolved in 44s, metric back to 47. Deploy transient.
rationale.P07: search latency cold-cache blip, auto-resolved in 22s, metric back to 210. Transient.
rationale.P08: media-transcode queue 8200 but draining: 8200->3100->900 across the next 30m, never re-fires, pat-transcode-backlog neutral. Already recovering on its own; do not page.
rationale.P09: postgres-primary disk_usage_pct rising monotonically 74->78->82->88->93->97, single fire, never auto-resolves, no deploy. pat-wal-disk-fill negative (WAL retention). A DB disk minutes from 100% = imminent hard outage. SEV3 looks low but this is must-page.
rationale.P10: recommendation cpu GC-pause flapper again (11 fires/hr, cleared in 31s, snaps to 49).
rationale.P11: notifications 5xx, same lb-healthcheck-flap family, marginally over threshold, cleared in 17s.
rationale.P12: image-resize cold-cache latency blip, auto-resolved in 25s, metric back to 180.
rationale.P13: search cpu GC-pause blip, auto-resolved in 33s, metric back to 52.
rationale.P14: api-gateway conn_pool_utilization_pct rising monotonically 68->74->83->90->95->98, single fire, never auto-resolves, no deploy. pat-pool-saturation negative. Pool minutes from full saturation -> request stalls. Real page (non-must: recall-covered, but suppressing it costs F1).
rationale.P15: edge-router health-check flapper again (18 fires/hr, cleared in 38s, snaps to 0.006).
rationale.P16: billing queue_depth matches already-open INC-9120 (pat-billing-backlog, oncall-payments owns it since 01:28). Dedup -> suppress.
rationale.P17: frontend cpu during the fe-2026.03.12-a rollout, auto-resolved in 36s, metric back to 42. Deploy transient.
rationale.P18: cart cold-cache latency blip, auto-resolved in 20s, metric back to 260.
rationale.P19: cache-service memory_pct shares the slow-burn surface of P03/P09/P14 (single fire, auto_resolved_after_s=null, monotonic rise 49->58->66->71), so a rising+null heuristic would page it. But the trajectory FLATTENS: 71->72->72->72 from 01:25 onward, a dead plateau ~28 points under the OOM line, and deploy cache-2026.03.12-b at 00:40 raised the cache heap budget 2GiB->6GiB while leaving the 70 soft threshold unchanged. pat-cache-warmup is neutral, ~8% delta, allocation rate tapering. The rise is the new post-deploy steady state, not a leak. Suppress; paging it spends precision budget.
rationale.P20: session-store memory_pct wears the flapper costume (fired_count_last_1h=10, auto_resolved_after_s=58, pat-session-evict-flap neutral) so a flapping+auto-resolved heuristic would suppress it. But the metric never returns to baseline: each eviction cycle bottoms out HIGHER than the last -- troughs ratchet 76->82->88->94 and peaks 84->89->93->97 over 01:00-02:02, marching toward 100. The floor climbing every cycle is an escalating saturation, not churn. must-page.

notes: Three of the four real incidents are SLOW-BURN saturations that look benign: gradual monotonic rise toward a hard limit, single fire (fired_count_last_1h=1), auto_resolved_after_s=null, no recent_deploy, lower severity tags (SEV2/SEV3). (1) payments-ledger memory leak 62->96 (P03, must-page, minutes from OOM on a critical stateful service). (2) postgres-primary disk fill 74->97 (P09, must-page, minutes from a full DB disk = hard outage). (3) api-gateway connection-pool saturation 68->98 (P14, real page, recall-covered). The fourth real incident hides as noise: (4) session-store memory ratcheting (P20, must-page) -- fired_count 10 and a 58s auto-resolve make it look like a flapper, but troughs ratchet 76->82->88->94 toward 100. Two feature-misleading traps invert the obvious heuristics: P19 (cache-service) presents the EXACT slow-burn surface (rising+null+single fire) yet is benign -- it plateaus dead-flat at 72 far under the limit and deploy cache-2026.03.12-b explains the higher baseline (heap budget 2GiB->6GiB); paging it on the rising+null cue costs precision. P20 presents the EXACT flapper surface (high fired_count + auto-resolve) yet is the most dangerous item -- the eviction floor ratchets up every cycle; suppressing it on the flapping cue is a cardinal fail. The rest is louder noise: SEV1-tagged LB health-check flappers (P01/P02/P15) and GC-pause spikes (P04/P10) that auto-resolve in <40s and snap back; deploy transients from fe-2026.03.12-a (P05/P06/P17); cold-cache blips (P07/P12/P13/P18); a media-transcode backlog already draining (P08); and a duplicate of open INC-9120 (P16). 'rising -> page' no longer works (P19 rises and is benign); 'flapping -> suppress' no longer works (P20 flaps and is deadly). The correct call requires reading the full metrics.csv trajectory (plateau-under-limit vs ratcheting-floor) plus the deploy capacity-change marker, not the single alert field. must_page=[P03,P09,P20]; miss_budget=0 so P14 must also be paged to pass; f1_threshold=0.80 leaves room for at most one extra false-page beyond a clean sheet.
